design_philosophy.core_loop: Select punches and defensive maneuvers with precise timing; build stamina between rounds; progress through ranked opponents to championship; improve boxer stats (punch, stamina, speed) with power points earned from victories
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward timing on special attacks—instant knockouts when executed correctly but massive damage if countered; stamina management as penalty for missed punches
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rise from debut boxer to world champion through training and strategic combat mastery; customize and develop a personal boxer's fighting style
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy emphasis on comical, exaggerated special moves (spinning hook, rope-propelling straight, airborne uppercut) over realistic boxing; encourages high-risk timing reads rather than grinding. Password-based progression allows player agency in boxer customization and advancement pacing

difficulty.checkpointing: Matches serve as progressive checkpoints; password system allows explicit save/load of boxer state and progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High—player can customize boxer stats (punch, stamina, speed) between matches using earned power points; perform well to create stronger boxers for next attempts

progression.upgrade_system: Three-stat boxer development system: punch, stamina, speed. Power points earned after each match used to improve stats. Performing well in matches enables creation of more powerful boxers for subsequent playthroughs
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear opponent ladder: Violence Jo → Brown Pants → White Wolf → Bomba Vern → Beat Brown → Blue Warker → Green Hante → Onetta Yank. After championship, arcade cycles through final three opponents
replayability_hooks: Two-player versus mode for head-to-head boxing
Password system encourages experimentation with different boxer stat configurations
Stat customization creates incentive to replay and optimize builds

world_building_techniques: Opponent naming conventions establish comedic, eccentric boxing culture (Violence Jo, Brown Pants, White Wolf, Bomba Vern, Beat Brown, Blue Warker, Green Hante, Onetta Yank)
Career progression arc from debut to world champion implies implicit rise-to-glory narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Quirky, thematically-named opponents with escalating difficulty; champion opponent serves as final skill check before cycling through top contenders. Design encourages pattern learning and special move timing mastery
notable_bosses: Violence Jo (entry-level champion in NES version)
Blue Warker (reigning champion in arcade version; final cycling opponent)

faction_indicators: Boxer stat customization creates distinct 'build archetypes' (speed-focused, power-focused, balanced stamina)
Two-player mode enables direct competition and playstyle differentiation
danger_signals: Special attack timing windows create skill gatekeeping—new players vulnerable to counter-damage
Stamina depletion from missed punches punishes execution errors heavily
environmental_storytelling: Regional logo changes (Namcot to Data East on ring floor) reflect publishing territories and commercial interests
Menu corrections across versions (MATCH→MATCHES, grammatical standardization) indicate localization care

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Ring King (NES)
